reasoning_chain_text: 1. Identify issuing agencies in the top 20 by number of January 2019 parking violations.
2. From those agencies, keep the issuing agencies that again appear in the top 20 by number of January 2020 parking violations.
3. From those agencies, keep the issuing agencies that again appear in the top 20 by number of January 2021 parking violations.
4. Among the agencies from that common set whose names start with United States or U.S., identify which issued the fewest but nonzero January 2019 moving violations.
5. Determine which federal agency manages the areas where that police force operates.
6. Determine which cabinet department that federal agency belongs to.
7. Determine the headquarters city of that department.
8. Return the river that city is located along.
original_final_question: In January 2019-2021, find the issuing agencies that appear in the top 20 by number of parking violations. Restricting to federal agencies, find the agencies issued the fewest yet nonzero moving violations in Jan 2019. That agency operates in National Park Service areas. Identify the department this agency is within, then identify the city where that department is headquartered. Finally, name the river this city is located along. Answer with the river name only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
original_reasoning_chain: Nodes 1–3: Find the top 20 January parking-violation issuing agencies for 2019, 2020, and 2021.
Node 4 depends on nodes 1–3: Among federal agencies common to all three lists, select the one with the fewest (nonzero) January 2019 moving violations (UNITED STATES PARK POLICE).
Node 5 depends on node 4: Identify the agency whose areas the United States Park Police primarily serves (National Park Service).
Node 6 depends on node 5: Identify the cabinet department that the National Park Service is within (United States Department of the Interior).
Node 7 depends on node 6: Identify the headquarters city of the Department of the Interior (Washington, D.C.).
Node 8 depends on node 7: Identify the river Washington, D.C. is situated on (Potomac River).
